channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: ‘Starfleet Academy’ Puts Itself on Trial, and Finds Its Old Generations Wanting
item.description: 'Rubincon' ends the first season of 'Starfleet Academy' with an interesting twist on its belief in the next generation—with some repuditation of the ones that came before them.

item.title: Researchers Say AI Is Homogenizing Human Expression and Thought
item.description: For a "large" language model, the diversity of outputs is pretty small.

item.title: Buy now or wait? SD card prices are shooting through the roof – here’s how to navigate the ‘memory crisis’
item.description: A global 'memory crisis' has seen the price of SD cards shoot up in 2026, typically by over 200%. Here's my buying advice, and which genuine models I still recommend

item.title: What it was like to watch grieving parents stare down Mark Zuckerberg in court
item.description: Around a dozen parents huddled in the dim hallway outside the courtroom in February, nervously gripping paper tickets. They were glaring at a gray tote bag held by a member of the court staff - the one who'd determine, by lottery, if they made it inside. Pinned on bags and coats, butterfly clips honored children […]

item.title: Save £150 on this 32GB Asus Core 7 mini PC in Amazon’s Spring Sale
item.description: Save over £150 on this Asus Core 7 mini PC with 32GB DDR5 and Windows 11 Pro in Amazon's Spring Deal Days sale

item.title: The original AirTag is the cheapest it’s ever been
item.description: Despite the fact Apple released a new AirTag in January, the first-generation AirTag is still a top-notch tracker if you’re embedded in Apple’s ecosystem. And right now, it’s on sale for $13.91 ($15.09 off) at Walmart, which marks a new low price. If you’re an iPhone owner, the original AirTag still delivers a level of […]

item.title: Amazon Employees Say AI Is Just Increasing Workload. A New Study Confirms Their Suspicions
item.description: Silicon Valley promised AI would make work less intense. Employees would beg to differ.

item.title: Meta is testing clickable links in Instagram captions for verified subscribers
item.description: Instagram has long limited users' ability to share links, restricting link-sharing to Stories, Reels and user profiles. But that might now be changing. The company has started to test clickable links inside of post captions for subscribers to Meta Verified. The new feature, which has been a long-requested update from creators, was spotted by blogger Andrea Valeria, who posted screenshots of a clickable Substack link she was able to add to an Instagram post. According to Valeria, an in-app message indicated she could share up to 10 links a month.Meta confirmed to Engadget that it's testing link...

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters is getting a sequel, obviously
item.description: Get ready to get even more K-pop, demonier, and as huntery as possible. Netflix's smash-hit, Oscar-nominated animated film KPop Demon Hunters is returning for a sequel, with the fictional Korean girl group HUNTR/X coming back to lay down pop hits and smash evil boy bands (and/or other demons). Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans will once […]

item.title: PEGI ratings for game releases in Europe will be age-restricted if they contain loot boxes
item.description: European regulators are continuing to crack down on loot boxes and gaming features it classifies as "interactive risk categories." The Pan-European Game Information, better known as PEGI, is rolling out new rules that will apply age ratings based on the presence of loot boxes and other in-game purchases or systems that could be tied to gambling or addictive behavior. The exact policies are as follows:Purchases of in-game content: games with time-limited or quantity-limited offers will be classified with a PEGI 12, games with NFTs or blockchain-related mechanisms will be PEGI 18.Paid random ite...

item.title: Amazon Spring Sale: This £92 HP 100Hz IPS monitor is the perfect budget upgrade for your home office
item.description: HP’s 23.8-inch business monitor has a 100Hz refresh, vibrant colour, a height-adjustable stand, and a £58 saving in the Amazon Spring Deal Days sale.

item.title: Netflix Makes the ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Sequel You Knew Was Coming Official
item.description: As the smash-hit animated film prepares to (probably) win an Oscar (or two), the streamer has confirmed Huntr/x will return.

item.title: Women are less likely to apply for jobs with a huge pay range. Here’s what companies can do about it
item.description: New research out of Cornell University indicates that pay transparency laws have not been as effective as intended—in part because many employers fail to truly comply with them. Pay transparency laws were supposed to address the pay disparities that tend to impact women and people of color in the workplace. Over the last decade, 15 states have introduced laws that require varying degrees of disclosure from employers—from including explicit salary ranges in job postings to verbally sharing those details with prospective employees during the interview process. ...

item.title: Uber says you can request champagne in its new Elite rides. Here’s how the service works
item.description: The new luxury chauffeur service requires riders to schedule trips at least one hour in advance and as far as 90 days ahead. Fancy a chauffeur? Uber is courting the well-heeled with a new ride option that will see it extend its reach from a taxi alternative to offering a more exclusive, limousine-style service.

item.title: ‘No Michelin stars for violence’: What’s happening with Noma? Rene Redzepi resigns amid protests and bombshell allegations
item.description: The celebrity chef of the iconic Copenhagen spot is facing serious accusations from former employees. René Redzepi, the chef behind Copenhagen’s Noma, has resigned from the iconic restaurant he co-founded and its food non-profit MAD, amid abuse allegations. The move comes after protesters gathered outside Noma’s 16-week Los Angeles pop-up Wednesday....

item.title: Substack launches a built-in recording studio
item.description: Substack creators can pre-record video conversations with up to two guests, then publish directly on the platform.

item.title: Gemini Is Now Your Permanent Passenger in Google Maps
item.description: Hopefully it doesn't hallucinate directions.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
item.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

item.title: Bumble is the latest dating app to add an AI assistant
item.description: Bumble is testing an AI dating assistant called "Bee" that it hopes will get users on dates without them having to swipe through profiles, Bloomberg writes. The company announced the AI assistant during its fourth quarter earnings, and intends to use the AI in a new experience it calls "Dates."When a user opts in to Bumble's Dates feature, Bee performs an onboarding chat where it learns about the users' "values, relationship goals, communications style, lifestyle and dating intentions," and then attempts to find other users who share some or all of those traits. Once Bee finds someone compatib...

item.title: Tesla wins energy licence to supply your electricity in the UK — but experts don't think it'll get a smooth ride
item.description: UK energy regulator grants Tesla license to supply electricity, as it moves beyond EVs and into the energy market.

item.title: Phoenix has lived with Waymos longer than any U.S. city. Here’s what its mayor learned
item.description: Mayor Kate Gallego talks about working with Waymo, redesigning cities for autonomous vehicles, and why robotaxis may reshape everything from parking to public transit. In 2020, Waymo began offering fully driverless rides to the public in Phoenix, turning the city into the closest thing the U.S. has to a real-world laboratory for autonomous vehicles. What began as a cautious pilot has since grown into a sprawling robotaxi network that now includes freeway travel and service to Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport....

item.title: Google Is Not Ruling Out Ads in Gemini
item.description: WIRED spoke with Nick Fox, Google’s SVP of knowledge and information, about how AI is changing the company’s advertising business.

item.title: Comfortable and more affordable: Steelcase's Amia ergonomic office chair is nearly £300 off for Amazon's Spring Deal Days sale
item.description: Steelcase's Amia ergonomic office chair is nearly £300 off for Amazon's Spring Deal Days sale.

item.title: Galaxy A37 et A57 : la douche froide sur les prix est confirmée pour la France
item.description: On sait enfin combien vont coûter les prochains best-sellers de Samsung. Les Galaxy A37 et A57 arrivent en France le 10 avril 2026 avec une mauvaise surprise.

item.title: Why smaller portions are the biggest restaurant trend right now
item.description: Some restaurants are even adding menus to court users of GLP-1 drugs. The biggest new restaurant trend is small.

item.title: 'The prevailing wisdom used to be that macOS was at lower risk of malware infection compared to Windows...that’s no longer the case': Experts warn Mac infostealers are on the rise - here's how to stay safe
item.description: Three distinct campaigns recently spotted by Sophos, all targeting MacOS users with infostealers.

item.title: MacBook Neo: Heres everything reviewers didn’t like
item.description: Here's a look at some of the drawbacks to Apple's newest laptop, the MacBook Neo.

item.title: DJI Avata 360 : voici la fiche technique monstrueuse pour un drone qui s’annonce lourd
item.description: DJI se décide enfin à regarder partout en même temps. Avec l'Avata 360, la marque chinoise répond à l'offensive d'Insta360 avec des arguments techniques.

item.title: A Green Lantern Is Joining ‘Man of Tomorrow,’ but Not the One You’d Expect
item.description: Aaron Pierre's John Stewart, soon to be seen on DC's HBO Max series 'Lanterns,' will be a part of James Gunn's next Superman film.

item.title: Soundcore Nebula P1i projector review: An affordable option with accurate color and loud sound
item.description: Anker’s Soundcore projectors have become an attractive option for buyers thanks to models like the P1 and Nebula X1 that combine performance and portability. Now, the company has added "affordability" to that equation with its latest model, the $369 P1i. Instead of being detachable like on the P1, its speakers fold out toward listeners, promising better and louder sound than most cheap projectors. The P1i also delivers 1080p video, Google TV for streaming and the same easy screen fit setup as other Anker projectors. However, unlike some portable models, it lacks a built-in battery for true por...

item.title: US Lawmakers Move to Kill the FBI’s Warrantless Wiretap Access
item.description: A bipartisan bill would force the FBI to get a warrant to read Americans’ messages and ban the federal purchase of commercial data on US residents ahead of a critical April deadline.

item.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
item.description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to re-engange its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

item.title: Quince copied its way to a $10 billion empire. Now it’s looking for a new story
item.description: The dupe-culture darling just raised $500 million. I ask its first head of brand and narrative how she’s positioning the brand for hyper growth. There’s a $298 midi dress on Reformation’s website with delicate lace detailing throughout and a button front that allows you to show some leg—it’s the kind of dress the brand is known for, versatile and a little seductive. On Quince, there’s what appears to be the same dress: It has the same silhouette, the same fabric, the same drape. The Quince version costs $69.90....

item.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
item.description: Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to those annoying "Is this still available?" messages. You can toggle on the auto-reply option when creating a listing, and Meta AI […]

item.title: Le prix des composants flambe, mais ce laptop Lenovo avec 24 Go de RAM et 1 To de SSD chute à 549 €
item.description: Le Lenovo IdeaPad Slim 3 15IRH10, doté d’un processeur i5 de 13ᵉ génération, 24 Go de RAM et 1 To de SSD, voit son prix chuter de 799 euros à seulement 549 euros sur Cdiscount. Une affaire particulièrement intéressante dans un contexte où la RAM et les SSD voient leurs prix flamber.
